inputs_received.persona: SDR in North America, owns Leads + Opportunities, uses Sales Console, needs Reports/Dashboards (read-only on non-personal data), cannot export reports, no access to Cases or Knowledge.

summary: SDR persona resolves to a four-permission-set PSG: one App PS (Sales Console + UI), one Object PS (Lead + Opportunity CRUD with no Delete on Opp), one Feature PS (Reports/Dashboards run-only, no Export), and one explicit deny PS (no Cases / no Knowledge / no Modify All Data). No near-match Feature PSes exist in the org per the probe stub, so a fresh PS is justified. Naming follows the App_/Obj_/Feat_/Deny_ prefix convention and the PSG composition order is Object → Feature → App → Deny so the deny set wins on conflict.
confidence_rationale: Persona is unambiguous and the fixture-supplied probe shows no near-match Feature PSes, but the harness lacks a live org probe — the 'no near-match' claim is library-only, capping confidence at MEDIUM per AGENT.md.

deliverables.title: App PS — SDR Sales Console
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<PermissionSet xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <label>App: SDR Sales Console</label>
    <description>Grants the LightningSales console app and the Sales Console UI surface for the SDR persona. Composition unit only — never assigned directly.</description>
    <hasActivationRequired>false</hasActivationRequired>
    <license>Salesforce</license>
    <applicationVisibilities>
        <application>standard__LightningSales</application>
        <visible>true</visible>
    </applicationVisibilities>
</PermissionSet>

deliverables.title: Object PS — SDR Lead + Opportunity CRUD
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<PermissionSet xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <label>Obj: SDR Lead + Opportunity</label>
    <description>Object/FLS surface for the SDR persona — full CRUD on Lead, CRU (no Delete) on Opportunity. Composition unit only.</description>
    <hasActivationRequired>false</hasActivationRequired>
    <license>Salesforce</license>
    <objectPermissions>
        <object>Lead</object>
        <allowCreate>true</allowCreate>
        <allowRead>true</allowRead>
        <allowEdit>true</allowEdit>
        <allowDelete>true</allowDelete>
        <viewAllRecords>false</viewAllRecords>
        <modifyAllRecords>false</modifyAllRecords>
    </objectPermissions>
    <objectPermissions>
        <object>Opportunity</object>
        <allowCreate>true</allowCreate>
        <allowRead>true</allowRead>
        <allowEdit>true</allowEdit>
        <allowDelete>false</allowDelete>
        <viewAllRecords>false</viewAllRecords>
        <modifyAllRecords>false</modifyAllRecords>
    </objectPermissions>
</PermissionSet>

deliverables.title: Feature PS — Reports + Dashboards run, no export
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<PermissionSet xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <label>Feat: Reports Run No Export</label>
    <description>Run-only Reports + Dashboards. Explicitly omits Export Reports and Manage Reports.</description>
    <hasActivationRequired>false</hasActivationRequired>
    <license>Salesforce</license>
    <userPermissions>
        <name>RunReports</name>
        <enabled>true</enabled>
    </userPermissions>
</PermissionSet>

deliverables.title: Deny PS (muting) — SDR boundaries
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<MutingPermissionSet xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <label>Deny: SDR Boundaries</label>
    <description>Removes Cases, Knowledge, and dangerous system perms even if a future PS in the SDR PSG adds them by accident.</description>
    <objectPermissions>
        <object>Case</object>
        <allowRead>false</allowRead>
        <allowCreate>false</allowCreate>
        <allowEdit>false</allowEdit>
        <allowDelete>false</allowDelete>
    </objectPermissions>
    <objectPermissions>
        <object>Knowledge__kav</object>
        <allowRead>false</allowRead>
    </objectPermissions>
    <userPermissions>
        <name>ExportReport</name>
        <enabled>false</enabled>
    </userPermissions>
    <userPermissions>
        <name>ModifyAllData</name>
        <enabled>false</enabled>
    </userPermissions>
</MutingPermissionSet>

deliverables.title: PSG — SDR (composition + ordering)
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<PermissionSetGroup xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <label>PSG: SDR</label>
    <description>Composes the four SDR-persona permission sets. Muting set runs last so deny wins on conflict. Assign this PSG to SDR users instead of any of its members directly.</description>
    <permissionSets>Obj_SDR_LeadOpportunity</permissionSets>
    <permissionSets>Feat_Reports_RunNoExport</permissionSets>
    <permissionSets>App_SDR_SalesConsole</permissionSets>
    <mutingPermissionSets>Deny_SDR_Boundaries</mutingPermissionSets>
    <status>Updated</status>
</PermissionSetGroup>

deliverables.title: Deployment order
deliverables.content:
# SDR Permission Set Group — deployment order

1. Deploy the four base permission sets in any order: `Obj_SDR_LeadOpportunity`, `Feat_Reports_RunNoExport`, `App_SDR_SalesConsole`, `Deny_SDR_Boundaries`.
2. Deploy `PSG_SDR` referencing all four (the last is the muting set).
3. Trigger PSG recalculation in the org (Setup → Permission Set Groups → Recalculate).
4. Assign `PSG_SDR` to a single canary SDR user. Verify in the UI that Cases is not visible and that the Export Report button is hidden in the Reports tab.
5. Bulk-assign to the SDR pod via `assign_perm_set_group` (or the Setup UI).

## Why one App PS, one Object PS, one Feature PS, one Deny PS
This is the canonical four-tile composition from `templates/admin/permission-set-patterns.md`: keep object/FLS, feature, app-launcher, and deny concerns in separate PSes so future personas can reuse units (e.g. an AE persona will reuse `Feat_Reports_RunNoExport` verbatim).

process_observations.observation: Org currently has 6 PSGs against 240 active users — well under the typical complexity threshold where Profile/PSG sprawl starts to bite.

Persona says 'no access to Cases or Knowledge' but does not say whether SDRs ever click into a Case from a converted Lead's account; if they do, an Object-level Read on Case may be needed and the deny set adjusted.
Once PSG_SDR is assigned to a real SDR pod, run a sharing audit to confirm record-level access matches the persona's North America scope (role hierarchy + sharing rules, not the PS).
process_observations.followup_reason: because permission sets grant object/field access but record-level scoping (NA-only) lives in the role hierarchy and sharing rules — out of scope for this agent.

citations.used_for: Four-tile PSG composition pattern (App + Object + Feature + Deny) and the rule that PSGs are the assignment unit, never raw PSes.
Muting permission set semantics — the deny set runs last and overrides additive grants.
Permission set XML scaffolds and the App_/Obj_/Feat_/Deny_ prefix convention.

followups.because: because permission sets grant object/field access but the persona's North America scoping must be enforced via role hierarchy + sharing rules.
because if a future field is added to Lead or Opportunity, the SDR PSG's FLS surface needs to be re-checked rather than assumed.
